articles.title: DOJ admits ICE courthouse arrests relied on erroneous information
articles.summary: Hundreds of immigrants have been arrested at immigration courthouses. It is unclear whether the federal government's admission could lead to some of those arrests being overturned.

articles.title: U.S. Stocks Have Their Biggest Drop Since Start of Iran War
articles.summary: The S&P 500 tumbled 1.7 percent on Thursday and oil prices continued rising as investors fret about when the war would end.

articles.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
articles.summary: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

articles.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
articles.summary: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

articles.title: Judge Questions Why U.S. Is Blocking Funding for Maduro’s Lawyers
articles.summary: The U.S. government has blocked Venezuela from paying for Nicolás Maduro’s defense lawyers. In a hearing, a judge said repeatedly that the former president’s right to his own defense was “paramount.”

articles.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
articles.summary: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

articles.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

articles.title: After Wooing Trump With Deals, Pakistan Gets a Seat at the Table
articles.summary: Steve Witkoff, a diplomatic envoy, used the Board of Peace to announce an agreement that could raze a Pakistan-owned Manhattan hotel. Now the country is involved in negotiating peace talks with Iran.

articles.title: Trump has deployed ICE agents to the nation's airports. What's their role?
articles.summary: The president says ICE agents are being stationed at airports to help reduce long wait times. Here's a look at what they're authorized to do.

articles.title: Europeans Worry Russia Is Preparing to Deliver Drones to Iran
articles.summary: The drones are an improved version of a weapon that Iran sent to Russia for use in its war in Ukraine.

articles.title: Safety Experts Considered LaGuardia Challenging but Not an Outlier
articles.summary: Regulators, pilots and others in aviation have worried about the kind of runway accident that happened at LaGuardia Airport on Sunday.

articles.title: Trump Administration Begins Investigations Into Three Medical Schools
articles.summary: The Justice Department’s demands for admissions-related data from Stanford, Ohio State and the University of California, San Diego, represent a flex of federal power.

articles.title: Trump Eyes White House Treaty Room for Latest Renovation Project
articles.summary: President Trump is making new plans in one of the most significant renovations in the history of the White House.

articles.title: America's first AI-fueled war is unfolding right now in Iran. This is how we got here
articles.summary: Bloomberg journalist Katrina Manson discusses the Pentagon's secretive campaign to build America's AI warfare capabilities and the obsessive Marine colonel behind it. Her new book is Project Maven.

articles.title: South Dakota Governor Signs Bill Requiring Citizenship Proof to Vote
articles.summary: The law, which mirrors national Republican priorities, requires newly registered voters to show that they are U.S. citizens in order to cast a ballot in state or local races.

articles.title: Suit asks court to force Trump administration to use 'The Kennedy Center' name
articles.summary: The motion is part of a lawsuit challenging President Trump and the Center's board, who now refer to the complex as "The Trump Kennedy Center."

articles.title: Bill Maher is getting the Mark Twain Prize after all
articles.summary: There was confusion about whether the satirist would be getting the Kennedy Center's top humor award after White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt called it "fake news." Now it's confirmed.

articles.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

articles.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
articles.summary: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

articles.title: Trump grants Iran another extension on a deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump said he'll hold off on striking Iran's power plants until April 6, postponing a deadline for Iran to let ships transit safely through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
articles.summary: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
